questions.question: How can you see where a collaborator is currently looking or working on a page?

options.text: Their cursor will appear in real-time
options.explanation: While cursors appear, the text explicitly states that profile photos indicate location, not just the cursor itself.

options.text: Their profile photo will appear on the page
options.explanation: The article states that profile photos show you where they are looking or working on the page.

options.text: A colored line will appear next to their name
options.explanation: Colored lines are not mentioned in the article; profile photos are the indicator.

options.text: You will receive a notification in your Inbox
options.explanation: Inbox notifications are for mentions and changes, not for seeing where someone is looking.

questions.question: What visual cue indicates that content has been removed by a collaborator?

options.explanation: Blue text highlights content that has been added, not removed.
The article specifies a gray strikethrough for removed content, not red.
Gray strikethrough is used to show what has been removed during real-time collaboration.
Notion provides visual cues like gray strikethrough so you can see what was removed.

questions.question: How do you leave a comment on a specific sentence or block of text?

options.text: Click the 'Add comment' button above the title
options.explanation: That button is for commenting on the page itself, not specific text blocks.
Highlighting the text and selecting the 'Comment' option from the menu allows you to comment on that specific block.
Right-clicking the page brings up the general menu, but you must right-click the specific block to comment on it.
Double-clicking is not the action described; highlighting and selecting 'Comment' is the correct method.

questions.question: How can you leave a comment on an image or embed block?

options.explanation: Clicking the image generally opens it, not a comment menu.

options.text: Click the dots (•••) or vertical dots (⋮⋮) and select 'Comment'
options.explanation: The article notes you can click the `•••` (top right) or `⋮⋮` (left hover) menu to choose 'Comment'.

options.text: Drag and drop a comment box onto the image
options.explanation: There is no drag-and-drop method mentioned for adding comments to blocks.
The article explicitly states that you can also right-click to bring up the comment menu.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Update bar' (clock icon 🕘) at the top of a page?

options.text: To show the current time in your timezone
options.explanation: The clock icon is not a standard clock; it functions as an 'Update bar' to track changes.

options.text: To show who made specific changes and when
options.explanation: The article states the icon shows 'who made what changes and when'.

options.text: To indicate if anyone else is currently viewing the page
options.explanation: Profile photos indicate who is working on the page; the update bar indicates history.

options.text: To show the total number of collaborators online
options.explanation: Profile photos show who is online/working; the update bar shows history of edits.

questions.question: How do you notify a teammate to look at a specific point in a page?

options.text: Type '@' followed by their name anywhere on the page
options.explanation: The article explains that typing '@' followed by their name triggers a real-time search and a notification link.
While you can email a page, the specific Notion feature for getting attention is the '@' mention.

options.text: Send a private message via the Sidebar
options.explanation: Sidebar messages are for direct DMs, not page mentions.

options.text: Mention them in the 'Add comment' box only
options.explanation: You can mention them anywhere on the page, not just in the comment box.

questions.question: What does a red badge with a number next to the Inbox icon indicate?

options.explanation: System updates appear with a grey badge; the red badge is specifically for mentions.
The article states that if you have been mentioned, you will see a red badge with the number of mentions.

options.text: New pages have been created in the workspace
options.explanation: New page creation is not indicated by the Inbox badge; it is for updates and mentions.
Unsaved changes are indicated by a blinking cursor or save icon, not by the Inbox badge.

questions.question: Where is the Inbox located in the Notion interface?

options.text: In the top right corner of the screen
options.explanation: The Inbox is located in the top left sidebar.

options.text: In the top right corner of the screen
options.explanation: The Inbox is located in the top left sidebar.
The article explicitly states to click on 'Inbox' at the top of your left sidebar.
The Inbox is a separate navigation element in the sidebar, not inside the comment button.

questions.question: According to the article, what is the primary communication style Notion is built to support?

options.explanation: Notion is described as 'built for asynchronous communication' to cut down on meetings.
While you can comment in real-time, the article emphasizes asynchronous updates.
The article introduces the section by stating, 'Notion is built for asynchronous communication.'
The article focuses on in-app features like mentions and comments rather than email.

questions.question: When you click on an update in your Inbox, where does it take you?

options.text: To the home page of the workspace
options.explanation: Clicking an update takes you to the specific comment, mention, or edit in question.

options.text: To the profile page of the person who sent the update
options.explanation: It takes you to the content itself, not the user's profile.

options.text: To the specific comment, mention, or edit in question
options.explanation: The article states, 'Click on any of these updates to go directly to the comment, mention, or edit in question.'
Updates are links to content, not settings.

questions.question: What happens when you press Enter after typing '@' and a name?

options.explanation: Pressing Enter confirms the mention and creates the link.

options.text: A search menu appears and then the person is notified
options.explanation: The menu searches in real-time; pressing Enter confirms the selection and notifies the person.
Mentions do not turn red; they link to the person's profile.
Pressing Enter is required to finalize the mention and trigger the notification.
